goals: Expose all Phase 4 nodes as importable Python functions in the appropriate `comfy_diffusion` submodules.
Follow the existing lazy-import pattern (no top-level `comfy.*` / `torch` imports).
Maintain CPU-only test coverage so CI remains green with no GPU required.

userStories.title: Resize image + mask together (ResizeImageMaskNode)
userStories.description: As a developer writing an LTX i2v or ControlNet pipeline, I want a `resize_image_mask(image, mask, width, height, interpolation)` function so that I can resize both the input image and its mask to the required dimensions in a single call.

acceptanceCriteria.text: `comfy_diffusion.image.resize_image_mask(image, mask, width, height, interpolation="bilinear")` is callable.

userStories.title: Resize images by longer edge (ResizeImagesByLongerEdge)
userStories.description: As a developer preparing images for LTX i2v or LTX23 workflows, I want a `resize_images_by_longer_edge(images, size)` function so that the image's longer dimension is scaled to `size` while preserving aspect ratio.

acceptanceCriteria.text: CPU pytest passes with mocked node.

userStories.title: Create a solid-color blank image (EmptyImage)
userStories.description: As a developer building an LTX t2v, i2v, or LoRA pipeline, I want an `empty_image(width, height, batch_size, color)` function so that I can create a blank image tensor as a placeholder or starting frame.

acceptanceCriteria.text: CPU pytest passes with mocked node.

userStories.title: Evaluate a math expression (ComfyMathExpression)
userStories.description: As a developer building an LTX23 pipeline that requires dynamic parameter computation, I want a `math_expression(expression, variables)` function so that I can evaluate parameterised numeric expressions at runtime (e.g. deriving frame counts from duration and fps).

acceptanceCriteria.text: Returns a numeric value (int or float).
CPU pytest passes with mocked node.

userStories.title: Split video into frames + audio (GetVideoComponents)
userStories.description: As a developer running canny, depth, or pose ControlNet workflows, I want a `get_video_components(video)` function so that I can decompose a loaded video tensor into its frame images and audio track for per-frame processing.

acceptanceCriteria.text: Returns a `(images_tensor, audio)` tuple (matching the node's output).
CPU pytest passes with mocked node.

userStories.title: Inject guide frames for LTX ControlNet (LTXVAddGuide)
userStories.description: As a developer building an LTX canny, depth, or pose workflow, I want an `ltxv_add_guide(conditioning, image, mask, strength, start_percent, end_percent)` function so that I can attach guide-frame conditioning for spatially controlled video generation.

acceptanceCriteria.text: `comfy_diffusion.controlnet.ltxv_add_guide(conditioning, image, mask, strength, start_percent, end_percent)` is callable.
CPU pytest passes with mocked node.

userStories.title: Edge detection via Canny (Canny)
userStories.description: As a developer preparing control images for LTX canny-to-video, I want a `canny(image, low_threshold, high_threshold)` function so that I can generate Canny edge maps directly from a PIL/tensor image.

acceptanceCriteria.text: Returns an image tensor of the same spatial dimensions.
CPU pytest passes with mocked node.

userStories.title: Depth map conditioning via Lotus (LotusConditioning)
userStories.description: As a developer building an LTX depth-to-video pipeline, I want a `lotus_conditioning(model, image)` function so that I can apply Lotus-model depth conditioning to the generation.

acceptanceCriteria.text: CPU pytest passes with mocked node.

userStories.title: Override first sigma in sampler (SetFirstSigma)
userStories.description: As a developer composing an LTX depth workflow, I want a `set_first_sigma(sigmas, sigma_override)` function so that I can adjust the first noise level of the sampler schedule for depth conditioning.

acceptanceCriteria.text: CPU pytest passes with mocked node.

userStories.title: Invert an image (ImageInvert)
userStories.description: As a developer post-processing a depth map for LTX depth-to-video, I want an `image_invert(image)` function so that I can invert pixel values (1 − pixel) as required by the depth workflow.

acceptanceCriteria.text: CPU pytest passes with mocked node.

userStories.title: LLM-based LTX23 prompt generation (TextGenerateLTX2Prompt)
userStories.description: As a developer using an LTX23 workflow, I want a `text_generate_ltx2_prompt(text, model, ...)` function so that the model's internal LLM can expand or refine prompts for LTX23.

acceptanceCriteria.text: Returns a string or text-conditioning object as returned by the node.
CPU pytest passes with mocked node.

userStories.title: CPU-only test coverage for all new wrappers
userStories.description: As a CI system, I want pytest tests for every new function that mock out all `comfy.*` / `torch` dependencies, so that all tests pass on CPU with no model files present.

acceptanceCriteria.text: One test file per new function group (or added to existing test files that cover the same module).
Every new function is covered by at least one passing test.
`uv run pytest` exits 0 with no GPU required.

functionalRequirements.description: ** All new functions follow the `str | Path` annotation convention for any path parameters.
** No top-level `torch`, `comfy.*`, or `comfy_extras.*` imports in any modified module — all deferred to call time.
** All new functions have a docstring with parameter descriptions and a return-type annotation.

** `DWPreprocessor` (pose estimation) is **not** included — it lives in a third-party ComfyUI extension (`comfyui_controlnet_aux`) not present in the vendor submodule; it is deferred to a future iteration once that extension is vendored.
